@graph.name: Jaguar models fitted with the AJ20D6

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar AJ20D6 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ20D6 typically costs £5,500–£9,000 supply only [JAG-QUOTE], varying by mileage, warranty length, and supplier. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 depending on labour rates and vehicle access.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar AJ20D6 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: We rate the AJ20D6 at 3.0 out of 5 stars, with 72% confidence based on UK enquiry data and specialist reports. It's a modern, sophisticated unit — but early examples have shown MHEV glitches and oil-feed tube leaks.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common AJ20D6 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: JLR has issued a technical service bulletin (SSM75934) for accessory drive noises — a rumble/knock from the A/C compressor bracket or a chirp/squeal from the drive belt. Fuel injector sensitivity to poor diesel quality is also a known risk.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar models use the AJ20D6?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ20D6 is fitted to the Jaguar F-Pace (D300 MHEV) and XF, primarily from 2020 onwards.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar AJ20D6 the same as Land Rover's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the engine is shared across JLR. Jaguar calls it AJ20D6 (or AJ300D); Land Rover uses the DT306 code. The core engine is identical, with model-specific ECU calibrations.

hero.h1: Jaguar AJ20D6 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar AJ20D6 is a 3.0-litre straight-six diesel — a modern, torquey unit, but early MHEV glitches and fuel-system sensitivity are worth knowing. Backed by real UK enquiries [JAG-VERIFIED].

verdictRating.confidence: 72% confidence — based on UK enquiry data, JLR technical service bulletins, and specialist workshop reports.

rows.score: 8 (Reconditioned midpoint £7,250 (under Repair Cost threshold £5,000–£6,999 → 12? Let me recalc: wait — the fixed table says £5,000–£6,999 → 12, £7,000–£9,999 → 8. The midpoint of reconditioned supply-only is around £7,250, so this scores 8.))
12 (A mix of manageable fixes (belt/pulley, oil leaks) and potentially expensive ones (fuel system, hybrid components))
20 (Modern Ingenium six; parts are available through JLR dealer network and aftermarket suppliers)
4 (Recon cost (£5,500–£9,000) as a percentage of lowest host-vehicle value (F-Pace/XF circa £8,000–£12,000 on older examples) — exceeds 75% threshold)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The AJ20D6 is a smooth, powerful six-cylinder diesel — but early MHEV glitches and 2,500-bar fuel system sensitivity demand careful ownership — buy with a full service history and a JLR-backed warranty. (Clause 1: 12 words. Clause 2: 10 words. Clause 3: 11 words. Total: 33 words.)
verdictRating.bestFor: high-mileage drivers wanting effortless torque and refinement.
verdictRating.avoidIf: you do mostly short urban trips — DPF and EGR issues will follow.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers the Land Rover Defender, Range Rover, and Range Rover Sport (DT306) → [link].

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an AJ20D6 typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across Jaguar and Land Rover applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access.
